philippines.title: Philippines, Chile ink poultry trade agreement
philippines.description:
MANILA, Philippines – The Department of Agriculture (DA) has entered into a regionalization agreement with Chile to ensure uninterrupted poultry shipments from the South American nation. Through Department Circular No. 30, the DA recognized Chile’s regionalization for high pathogenicity avian influenza (HPAI), commonly known as bird flu. READ: Philippines inks poultry deal with Netherlands In turn, this effectively lifted the temporary import ban on Chilean domestic and wild birds and their products, including day-old chicks, eggs and semen. Through the agreement, the Philippine government will allow imports from disease-free areas and will no lift the country-wide import restriction. Furthermore, the […]...
Keep on reading: Philippines, Chile ink poultry trade agreement

philippines.title: PH woos biojet fuel investments
philippines.description:
MANILA, Philippines – The Philippines aims to play a bigger role in sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) as a production and investment hub. “Investing in SAF is investing in the future,” Trade Undersecretary Ceferino Rodolfo said in a statement. “The Philippines has all of the elements needed to be a global hub, a world leader when it comes to SAF.” READ: Philippines, Thailand most exposed to aviation slump — think tank To support that goal, the BOI has partnered with Island Skies Alliance (ISA), a nonprofit aviation organization that works with airlines, investors, technology providers, sustainability experts and government agencies involved in […]...
Keep on reading: PH woos biojet fuel investments

philippines.title: Drug suspect with M-14 rifle nabbed in Cotabato
philippines.description:
COTABATO CITY – Anti-narcotics operatives serving a search warrant arrested a suspect for possession of alleged shabu and a loose high-powered firearm in Kabacan, Cotabato on Wednesday. Charlene Magdurulang, Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency (PDEA-12) regional director, said the operation conducted in Sitio Lumayong, Barangay Kayaga led to the arrest of a 45-year-old man known by the alias “Sammy,” a resident of the area and identified as a member of private armed group Recovered during the operation were 20 heat-sealed transparent plastic sachets containing suspected shabu with a total estimated weight of approximately five grams and a street value of P34,000. […]...
Keep on reading: Drug suspect with M-14 rifle nabbed in Cotabato

energy.title: India’s Energy Import Bill Jumps 82% Due to High Oil Prices
energy.description: India’s imports of crude and LNG jumped in May from April, as arrivals of non-Middle Eastern cargoes accelerated, and the country's energy import bill soared by nearly 82% from a year earlier amid higher energy prices. India’s crude oil imports rose by 7.5%, and LNG imports jumped by 16% in May from the previous month, according to provisional data from the oil ministry compiled by Indian media. The country paid as much as $18.7 billion on oil and gas imports in May, up by 81.6% compared to the May 2025 energy import bill of $10.3 billion,…

energy.title: More Than 60 Million Barrels of Oil Ready to Head to Asia as Hormuz Reopens
energy.description: Crude cargo arrivals in Asia from the Middle East could accelerate in the coming weeks as more than 60 million barrels of oil stuck in the Persian Gulf prepare to exit the Strait of Hormuz and head to Asian markets once the chokepoint reopens to traffic.   About 62 million barrels of crude oil on nearly three dozen supertankers are expected to make their way to Asia within weeks after the Strait reopens, according to Signal Group data carried by Bloomberg. Asia, which felt the supply shock first and the most as early as in March, could now…
